title: Anisopappus alticola
taxonomy: Anisopappus alticola is classified under the family Asteraceae, commonly known as the daisy or sunflower family. It belongs to the genus Anisopappus, a group of flowering plants native to Africa. The species is a part of the tribe Inuleae, which comprises several genera of flowering plants. Further classification places Anisopappus alticola within the subfamily Asteroideae, a large and diverse group of flowering plants. The species is also categorized as a perennial herb, further defining its classification within the plant kingdom.

intro: Anisopappus alticola, also known as Mountain Anisopappus, is a species of flowering plant in the daisy family (Asteraceae). This plant is valued for its potential health benefits, including its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which may aid in the treatment of various ailments such as wound healing and respiratory infections. The bioactive constituents of Anisopappus alticola include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, which contribute to its medicinal properties. In traditional medicine, Anisopappus alticola is often prepared as a tea or infusion, which is also available in capsule or tincture form, allowing for its bioactive compounds to be accessed for potential therapeutic uses.
